English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Gretel Pelto papers
Pelto, Gretel H.
29-2-4327
24 cubic feet. 24 cubic feet.
1974-2017. In English. 10475256
Restrictions on Access
Access to collection is restricted until completion of processing.
Scope and content
Includes correspondence, reports, publications and research pertaining to Pelto's work in public health and nutritional anthropology and her career at the World Health Organization and in the Division of Nutritional Sciences, Cornell University.
Preferred Citation
Gretel Pelto Papers, # 29-2-4327. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Professor emeritus in the Division of Nutritional Sciences (1999-2006), Cornell University. Pelto received a BA in sociology (1963), MA (1967) and PhD (1970) in anthropology from the University of Minnesota. Her work bridges the interface between academic research and actions to improve nutrition and public health in communities, with an emphasis on infant and young child nutrition.
Biographical / Historical
Pelto began her teaching career in the Department of Nutritional Sciences at the University of Connecticut, doing field research in Mexico and Latin America and Finland, with support from a Fulbright grant. She served for eight years as a senior social scientist at the World Health Organization, responsible for creating research tools and implementing their application to improve household management of acute respiratory infection and diarrheal diseases in developing countries.
Related Materials
Comparing Preventive and Recuperative Approaches to Reducing Child Undernutrition
Related Materials
Human Nutrition Collaborative Research Support Program in Mexico
Related Materials
See also collections #23-2-2817, #29-1-3247 and #47-10-3394 for more biographical information.
